blocks.content:
A program often needs to show information to the user.

For example:

• a greeting message
• instructions
• a result of a calculation

In console programs the text appears in the console window.
In C# we use the command `Console.WriteLine` to print text in the console.
Let’s look at this line of code.
`Console` is the part of the program that works with the console window.
`WriteLine` is the command that tells the program to print something in the console.

`"Hello"` is the text that will appear in the console.

Text in C# must always be written inside quotation marks.
Retype this code and check it.

Write two lines of code.

First print the text "Hello".
Then print the number 25.

Use `Console.WriteLine`.

Important to remember:

Text in C# must always be written inside quotation marks.

Example:
`Console.WriteLine("Hello");`

If a word is written without quotation marks, the program will not treat it as text.
